{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Web / App Developer (NYSC)",
  "description": "TechSpace is a dynamic IT company dedicated to providing innovative solutions to businesses and organizations. Our team of skilled professionals is committed to delivering cutting-edge technology services tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. With a focus on excellence, integrity, and customer satisfaction, we strive to empower businesses with the tools they need to succeed in today&#39;s digital landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Web / App Developer (NYSC)

Job Location: Jabi, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: NYSC

Job Summary


	The Web Developer/ Mobile App will be responsible for creating professional and user-friendly websites or (and) Mobile Application ensuring proper functionality, implementing design principles to enhance the user experience. Training students on Web Design and (or) Mobile App Development.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree / HND in a relevant field with 1- 3 years work experience.
	General IT Knowledge
	Proficiency in web design / mobile App tools and technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP.etc.)
	Knowledge of responsive design principles
	Knowledge of any framework like Flutter, Dart, Firebase, Laravel, CodeIgniter, React JS, Node Js, etc.
	Creativity and Problem-Solving Skills
	Graphic Design skills
	Attention to detail
	Understanding of User Experience (UX) and User Interface (UI) principles
	Time management skills.


Salary
N50,000 / month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: hello@techspace.com.ng cc: techspacengn@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Web / App Developer (NYSC) Job at TechSpace




        
    

        
        TechSpace is a dynamic IT company dedicated to providing innovative solutions to businesses and organizations. Our team of skilled professionals is committed to delivering cutting-edge technology services tailored to meet the unique needs of our clients. With a focus on excellence, integrity, and customer satisfaction, we strive to empower businesses with the tools they need to succeed in today's digital landscape.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Web / App Developer (NYSC)

Job Location: Jabi, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: NYSC

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Web Developer/ Mobile App will be responsible for creating professional and user-friendly websites or (and) Mobile Application ensuring proper functionality, implementing design principles to enhance the user experience. Training students on Web Design and (or) Mobile App Development.


Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree / HND in a relevant field with 1- 3 years work experience.
	General IT Knowledge
	Proficiency in web design / mobile App tools and technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP.etc.)
	Knowledge of responsive design principles
	Knowledge of any framework like Flutter, Dart, Firebase, Laravel, CodeIgniter, React JS, Node Js, etc.
	Creativity and Problem-Solving Skills
	Graphic Design skills
	Attention to detail
	Understanding of User Experience (UX) and User Interface (UI) principles
	Time management skills.


Salary
N50,000 / month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] cc: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

The company works with international partners to bring the best in engineering and construction to Nigeria and to take the best of Nigerian engineering to the rest of the world.

Lead Pipeline Engineer Job at Offshore Dimensions Limited




        
    

        
        
     

            
    

Offshore Dimensions Limited is a Nigerian EPC company that is committed to building engineering and construction capabilities in Nigeria. The company is driven by outstanding professionals who are examples of the phenomenal Nigerian spirit and who consistently execute projects with innovativeness, passion and speed.

Our company’s focus is engineering and construction of offshore pipelines, floating and fixed facilities and subsea production systems. The company works with international partners to bring the best in engineering and construction to Nigeria and to take the best of Nigerian engineering to the rest of the world.

Currently, the company represents the following OEM'S In Nigeria as their Official Distributors, they are Parker Hannifin, Vishal Steel Nigeria, POK, Corderie dor and Stracau Valves.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Lead Pipeline Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time

Requirements


	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications with 12+ years of working experience. 


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inverter Installation, Commissioning & Maintenance Supervisor",
  "description": "Blue Wave New Energy Technology Nigeria Company Limited is an innovative new energy company headquartered in Nigeria, committed to providing economical and sustainable power energy solutions to the African continent. The company was founded in 2023 and is headquartered in Lagos, with R&amp;D and manufacturing centres in Jiangxi, China.

With cost-effective products and innovative business models, Blue Wave New Energy is committed to contributing clean and reliable power energy to Africa&#39;s progress and prosperity. Its product lines cover solar lighting systems, portable energy storage equipment, home-integrated energy storage systems, and outdoor mobile power platforms to meet customers&#39; power needs in multiple scenarios.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inverter Installation, Commissioning &amp; Maintenance Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly skilled, hands-on, and results-driven Inverter Installation, Commissioning &amp; Maintenance Supervisor to oversee solar inverter installations, commissioning, troubleshooting, and installation team performance.
	This role combines strong technical expertise with leadership and field coordination responsibilities to ensure every solar installation project meets the highest operational, safety, and quality standards.
	The ideal candidate must have prior experience working in a solar company and possess strong managerial and coordination abilities to supervise both in-house and contract technical engineers.


Key Responsibilities
Installation &amp; Commissioning:


	Install, commission, maintain, and troubleshoot: Off-grid and hybrid inverter systems of Residential and small commercial solar-storage systems
	Configure inverter parameters and lithium battery/BMS communication
	Conduct system testing, load verification, and performance checks
	Diagnose and resolve inverter faults, wiring issues, communication errors, and system instability.


Team Leadership &amp; Coordination:


	Supervise installation engineers, technicians, and electricians Coordinate in-house and contract technical teams
	Ensure compliance with installation SOPs and electrical safety standards
	Monitor installation quality, grounding, protection systems, and wiring standards


Technical Support:


	Provide technical support to customers and internal teams
	Handle after-sales technical issues and field troubleshooting
	Guide customers on system operation and maintenance best practices.


Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Prepare commissioning, maintenance, and fault reports
	Maintain proper technical and project documentation
	Provide feedback to improve installation processes and system reliability.


Requirements


	HND or B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering or related fields with 4 - 5 years of relevant experience in a solar company.
	Strong experience with off-grid and hybrid inverter systems
	Hands-on experience with lithium battery systems (48V / 51.2V preferred)
	Proven experience managing residential and commercial solar installations
	Strong troubleshooting and fault diagnosis skills
	Ability to independently manage installation and commissioning workflows
	Strong leadership, coordination, and communication skills.


Benefits


	Competitive salary structure
	Installation commissions
	Material commissions
	Operations allowance
	Performance bonuses
	HMO coverage (extends to family)
	Pension
	NSITF
	ITF
	Group Life Insurance
	Housing endowment allowance
	Collaborative and growth-focused work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@bluewave.top using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Inverter Installation, Commissioning & Maintenance Supervisor Job at Blue Wave New Energy Technology Nigeria Company Limited




        
    

        
        Blue Wave New Energy Technology Nigeria Company Limited is an innovative new energy company headquartered in Nigeria, committed to providing economical and sustainable power energy solutions to the African continent. The company was founded in 2023 and is headquartered in Lagos, with R&D and manufacturing centres in Jiangxi, China.

With cost-effective products and innovative business models, Blue Wave New Energy is committed to contributing clean and reliable power energy to Africa's progress and prosperity. Its product lines cover solar lighting systems, portable energy storage equipment, home-integrated energy storage systems, and outdoor mobile power platforms to meet customers' power needs in multiple scenarios.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inverter Installation, Commissioning & Maintenance Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking a highly skilled, hands-on, and results-driven Inverter Installation, Commissioning & Maintenance Supervisor to oversee solar inverter installations, commissioning, troubleshooting, and installation team performance.
	This role combines strong technical expertise with leadership and field coordination responsibilities to ensure every solar installation project meets the highest operational, safety, and quality standards.
	The ideal candidate must have prior experience working in a solar company and possess strong managerial and coordination abilities to supervise both in-house and contract technical engineers.


Key Responsibilities
Installation & Commissioning:


	Install, commission, maintain, and troubleshoot: Off-grid and hybrid inverter systems of Residential and small commercial solar-storage systems
	Configure inverter parameters and lithium battery/BMS communication
	Conduct system testing, load verification, and performance checks
	Diagnose and resolve inverter faults, wiring issues, communication errors, and system instability.


Team Leadership & Coordination:


	Supervise installation engineers, technicians, and electricians Coordinate in-house and contract technical teams
	Ensure compliance with installation SOPs and electrical safety standards
	Monitor installation quality, grounding, protection systems, and wiring standards


Technical Support:


	Provide technical support to customers and internal teams
	Handle after-sales technical issues and field troubleshooting
	Guide customers on system operation and maintenance best practices.


Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare commissioning, maintenance, and fault reports
	Maintain proper technical and project documentation
	Provide feedback to improve installation processes and system reliability.


Requirements


	HND or B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering or related fields with 4 - 5 years of relevant experience in a solar company.
	Strong experience with off-grid and hybrid inverter systems
	Hands-on experience with lithium battery systems (48V / 51.2V preferred)
	Proven experience managing residential and commercial solar installations
	Strong troubleshooting and fault diagnosis skills
	Ability to independently manage installation and commissioning workflows
	Strong leadership, coordination, and communication skills.


Benefits


	Competitive salary structure
	Installation commissions
	Material commissions
	Operations allowance
	Performance bonuses
	HMO coverage (extends to family)
	Pension
	NSITF
	ITF
	Group Life Insurance
	Housing endowment allowance
	Collaborative and growth-focused work environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Talent Towers
content:
Talent Towers - Founded with a clear purpose to transform the human resource landscape in Africa, Talent Towers is a people-first Human Capital Solutions firm committed to delivering innovative, practical, and impactful solutions that drive business performance and workforce excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Storekeeper

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

            
    




	We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Storekeeper to maintain the integrity of our physical assets and inventory.
	You will be the first line of defense in managing stock accuracy, ensuring that all goods, from incoming raw materials to finished products, are organized, secured, and accounted for with high precision.


Key Responsibilities


	Receive and Inspect: Verify the quality and quantity of all incoming goods against purchase orders or delivery notes.
	Organize and Store: Allocate space for goods systematically to ensure efficient retrieval and space utilization.
	Maintain Records: Update stock cards, registers, and digital inventory management systems (e.g., Excel/ERP) daily for every movement of stock.
	Rotation: Implement inventory rotation methods (e.g., FIFO - First In, First Out) to ensure stock freshness and reduce wastage.
	Stock Audits: Conduct regular physical stock counts (daily/weekly/monthly) and reconcile them with system records to identify and report variances.
	Low-Stock Alerts: Monitor stock levels and proactively notify management when items reach re-order points to prevent stock-outs.
	Documentation: Prepare goods received notes (GRN), requisition forms, and release notes with 100% accuracy.
	Access Control: Ensure the store is secured at all times and that only authorized personnel have access to restricted areas.
	Housekeeping: Maintain the warehouse in a clean, orderly, and safe state, adhering to all health and safety regulations.
	Asset Protection: Report any damaged or obsolete goods immediately and ensure proper disposal procedures are followed as directed.


Requirements


	Minimum of an OND, or equivalent in a relevant field
	2–3 years of experience in a storekeeping, warehousing, or inventory management role
	Basic proficiency in Microsoft Excel (data entry, simple formulas)
	Experience using Inventory Management Software/ERP is a strong plus
	Strong mathematical and numeracy skills for accurate tallying and counting
	Must be honest and reliable, as this role involves direct handling of valuable assets
	Ability to spot discrepancies in counts, labels, or product conditions


Salary
N200,000 Monthly Net.

Application Closing Date: 26th June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Maintenance Technician

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	We are looking for a skilled and proactive Maintenance Technician to serve as the backbone of our operational infrastructure.
	You will be responsible for the "behind-the-scenes" excellence of our facilities, ensuring that all kitchen equipment, electrical systems, plumbing, and building fixtures are in peak working condition.
	Your work ensures that our service team can deliver without interruption or equipment failure.


Key Responsibilities


	Equipment Maintenance: Perform routine maintenance and urgent repairs on kitchen equipment (e.g., industrial burners, fryers, refrigeration units, ovens) to ensure they operate at peak efficiency.
	Facilities Repairs: Manage general building repairs including electrical (lighting, sockets, wiring), plumbing (leaks, blockages, faucets), and basic carpentry/painting tasks to maintain a conducive dining ambience.
	Preventive Maintenance Schedule: Develop and execute a preventative maintenance calendar to identify and resolve potential equipment failures before they disrupt service.
	Safety & Compliance: Ensure all repairs comply with health and safety regulations, maintaining a safe environment for both staff and customers.
	Vendor Management: Supervise third-party contractors for major repairs (e.g., heavy HVAC or electrical overhaul) and ensure their work meets our quality and safety standards.
	Inventory & Resource Management: Maintain a well-organized inventory of spare parts and tools, minimizing downtime by ensuring necessary components are always on hand.
	Rapid Response: Provide "on-the-spot" troubleshooting for operational issues during peak business hours to ensure that "products are available on time" and service remains uninterrupted.


Requirements


	Technical Background: Trade certification (OND/Technical School Certificate) in Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related fields.
	Experience: 3–5 years of hands-on maintenance experience, preferably in a commercial kitchen, restaurant, or hotel environment.
	Versatility: Strong diagnostic skills across multiple trades (Electrical, Plumbing, HVAC/Refrigeration).
	Speed & Efficiency: Proven ability to work under pressure and troubleshoot problems quickly in a fast-paced setting.
	Integrity & Safety: A high sense of responsibility regarding safety standards and the ability to work independently with minimal supervision.
	Communication: Ability to log maintenance requests, report on repairs, and communicate effectively with restaurant management regarding equipment status.


Salary
N250,000 Monthly.

Application Closing Date: 23rd June, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Dental Nurse

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Helping with infection control by sterilizing and disinfecting instruments, setting up instrument trays, prepare materials, and assisting with dental procedures.
	Assisting dentists in managing medical and dental emergencies when necessary.
	Giving patients information on dental hygiene, oral health care, and plaque control programs.
	Preparing Patients for dental procedures.
	Operate technical dental machines in diagnosis and treatment of Patients
	Assist dentist in examining dental and oral health of patients
	Assist dentist in attending to Patients within the allocated time for waiting and treatment
	Comply with all Practices’ policies and procedure on diagnosis, treatment, etc.
	Input, update and keep accurate records of Patients’ dental structure.
	Take pictures of clients and procedures
	And any other assigned duties


Role Requirements


	Candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree qualification with at least 3 years of work experience.
	Ability to perform basic dental procedures
	Excellent customer service skills and communication skills
	Communication - Actively listens and checks for under- standing.
	Working knowledge of Photography
	Team Player - The ability to work with others in a way that recognises their contribution and strengths, promotes collaborative working across different or cross functional teams to achieve organisational objectives.


Application Closing Date: 23rd June, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Executive Secretary Job at Blossom Prince




        
    

        
        Blossom Prince was established in 2007 under KC auto investment registered with CAC n Nigeria .Blossom deal on house keeping products, toiletries, car care products and gift item. We have reliable partner in countries like USA, Indonesia, Malaysia and China. With our partnership we bring customised quality products with affordable prices in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Secretary
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Receive and screen phone calls and redirect them when appropriate
	Handle and prioritize all outgoing or incoming correspondence (e-mail, letters, packages etc.)
	Make travel arrangements for executives
	Handle confidential documents ensuring they remain secure
	Prepare invoices or financial statements and provide assistance in bookkeeping
	Monitor office supplies and negotiate terms with suppliers to ensure the most cost-effective orders
	Maintain electronic and paper records ensuring information is organized and easily accessible.


Salary
N100,000 - N300,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
